mission.title: opentofu: -exclude flag for targeted plan/apply
mission.description: -exclude flag for targeted plan/apply. Requested by 54+ users.

steps.title: Check current opentofu setup
steps.description:
Verify your opentofu version and configuration:
```bash
tofu version
```
This feature requires a working opentofu installation.

steps.title: Review opentofu configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant opentofu configuration:
This RFC details a new flag, `-exclude`, which would do the inverse of `-target` - that is, plan/apply all resources _except_ those specified by the exclude flag and their descendants.

steps.title: Apply the fix for -exclude flag for targeted plan/apply
steps.description:
> While I can appreciate these arguments, I don't support it. The user using the tool has better context of what they need, and a better understanding of their desire. I think we have smart users and we shouldn't complicate their lives by limiting what they can do. After all, why have -exclude in
```yaml
-exclude module.entire_environment \
  -include module.entire_environment.module.network \
  -include module.entire_environment.module.application \
  -exclude module.entire_environment.module.application.module.eks
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "-exclude flag for targeted plan/apply" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: > While I can appreciate these arguments, I don't support it. The user using the tool has better context of what they need, and a better understanding of their desire. I think we have smart users and we shouldn't complicate their lives by limiting what they can do.

prerequisites.description: A working opentofu installation or development environment.
